---
title: "1.10 Calling Class Methods"
subtitle: "Using Objects and Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Class Methods

### Static methods belong to the class

Most methods used so far in this part of the course are <span class="term">class methods</span>, also called <span class="term">static methods</span>.

```java
public static returnType methodName(parameters)
{
    // body
}
```

They are called with the class name, such as `Math.sqrt(25)`.

## Non-void Methods

### Some methods calculate and return a value

![Method as a function](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-10-calling-class-methods/function.png){fig-align="center" width="44%"}

<span class="term">void</span> methods do not return a value.  
<span class="term">non-void</span> methods do return a value that must match the declared return type.

## Square Example

### Return values come back to the caller

![Square method model](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-10-calling-class-methods/square-method.png){fig-align="center" width="46%"}

```java
public static int square(int number)
{
    int result = number * number;
    return result;
}
```

The <span class="term">return</span> statement ends the method and sends the value back.

## Using Returned Values

### Do something with the result

```java
int y = square(5);
System.out.println(square(4));
```

Common mistakes:

::: {.tight-list}
- ignoring the returned value
- storing it in the wrong type
- passing arguments in the wrong order
:::

With non-void methods, the returned value is the whole point.

## Methods Outside the Class

### Use the class name when the method lives elsewhere

If a method is in another class, call it with:

```java
ClassName.methodName(arguments)
```

Examples:

::: {.tight-list}
- `Math.sqrt(9)`
- `Math.pow(3, 2)`
:::

This topic is the bridge between custom examples like `square()` and library use like `Math`.

## Classroom Task

### Ladder on Tower

![Ladder on tower challenge](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-10-calling-class-methods/ladder.png){fig-align="center" width="30%"}

The retained coding challenge uses the Pythagorean theorem:

`c = sqrt(a^2 + b^2)`

It is a good checkpoint for:

::: {.tight-list}
- method calls with arguments
- return values
- `Math.sqrt` and `Math.pow`
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- define class methods as <span class="term">static</span> methods
- distinguish `void` and non-void methods
- explain the job of the `return` statement
- use or store a returned value correctly
- call methods in other classes with `ClassName.methodName(...)`
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
